steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Use the parperp shortcut: find radius slope
steps.content:
The radius connects center $(-4, -6)$ to tangent point $(-7, -7)$:
$$m_{\text{radius}} = \frac{-7 - (-6)}{-7 - (-4)} = \frac{-1}{-3} = \frac{1}{3}$$

steps.title: Take the negative reciprocal for the tangent slope
steps.content:
The tangent line is perpendicular to the radius, so its slope is:
$$m_{\text{tangent}} = -\frac{1}{1/3} = -3$$
A is correct.
